_comment: Pre-registered 20-dimension rubric. Do NOT edit after first scored run.

dimensions.rubric: The decomposition produces exactly three services aligned with the bounded contexts: Animals, Adoptions, and Donations.
A YARP gateway project exists and routes /api/* to the services.
Each service is a separate ASP.NET Core project with its own .csproj and Program.cs.
No service references another service's project directly; inter-service communication is HTTP, not project reference.
Each service owns a distinct DbContext class.
Schema isolation is enforced via HasDefaultSchema on each DbContext.
Connection strings use distinct Search Path settings per service.
Foreign keys that would cross bounded contexts in the monolith are replaced with identifier references (no cross-schema FKs).
Inter-service HTTP calls use typed HttpClient classes registered via IHttpClientFactory.
Internal endpoints used only for inter-service communication are routed under /internal/ and not exposed by the gateway.
Public endpoints follow REST conventions (GET /api/<resource>, POST /api/<resource>, GET /api/<resource>/{id}).
A docker-compose.yml at repo root brings up postgres + all services + gateway, with port assignments 5101/5102/5103/5100.
Each service has a Dockerfile that targets .NET 10 (mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:10.0).
appsettings.json contains no hardcoded production secrets.
AllowedHosts is set to a specific host, not '*', in every service's appsettings.Production.json (if present).
Internal endpoints are protected by a loopback or shared-key filter.
CORS policy is restrictive (no AllowAnyOrigin in production).
README.md at repo root documents how to run the system locally.
Each service has its own README or service-level documentation.
ARCHITECTURE.md or equivalent documents the service boundaries and inter-service communication contract.
